name: Qarib
title: Servant of Master of Martyrs
shortDescription: The loyal servant of Imam Hussain (AS) whose mother was the Imam's maid, and he grew up in the Ahl al-Bayt's house from childhood to sacrifice his life for his master.
fullStory:
Qarib ibn Abdullah al-Du'ali was the loyal and sincere servant of the Master of Martyrs (AS). His mother (Fakiha) was a maid whom Imam Hussain (AS) had purchased and then married to a man named Abdullah, the fruit of which was Qarib. Thus, Qarib grew up in the Imam's house from early childhood under the love of the Ahl al-Bayt.

When Imam Hussain (AS) decided to leave Medina and travel to Mecca and then Karbala, Qarib was his constant companion and at his service. Filled entirely with love for his master, he never separated from the Imam for a moment.

On the day of Ashura, although Qarib was considered a servant in terms of social status, his free-spirited nature and religious zeal were greater than many claimants of religiosity. He sought permission from the Imam to go to the battlefield.

Qarib shone brightly in the collective attack of the Kufan army that occurred on the morning of Ashura (the first attack). In defending the sanctuary's tents, he fought with all his might and made his chest a shield against the enemy's arrows.

After a fierce battle and repelling enemy attacks, this loyal servant was targeted by a cowardly barrage of arrows and was martyred, so that his name would be registered among the Imam's closest companions.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى قارِبٍ مَوْلى الحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Qarib, the servant of Hussain ibn Ali.
